evidence: The paper evaluated random forest as one of five candidate models but selected LASSO as the optimal final model: "Among the five machine learning algorithms evaluated (random forest, Cox regression, LASSO, generalized linear models, and support vector machines), the LASSO-based model demonstrated superior predictive performance." The paper reports "regression coefficients of the 51 proteins in the LASSO-based model" (L1-regularized weights, not forest impurity). Statistical inference relies on Cox regression p-values and held-out test set validation (AUC=0.835, C-index=0.829), not ranger importance p-values or impurity-based feature importance.
